name: DOGE Asymmetric Trend Persistence v1
thesis: Choppy DOGE 4h markets can still reward continuation if trigger, risk, and exit roles are separated instead of overloaded onto one indicator. The edge should come from short persistence bursts with asymmetric exits and fast de-risk when follow-through stalls.
edge_mechanism: trend persistence with asymmetric exit structure
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, internally contradictory, or cannot produce durable post-cost edge in blind windows.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Short-lived persistence bursts that emerge even inside otherwise choppy price action.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: DOGE often produces brief momentum phases inside chop; asymmetric management can exploit those without requiring a long sustained trend.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h has enough impulsiveness for persistence bursts while remaining liquid enough for repeated tests.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If momentum bursts are too short or too rare, the persistence trigger will still under-participate.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is low but not zero, relax the ADX and structure thresholds before changing exits.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; persistence and structure roles are separated but compatible
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by modest ADX floor and broad hold geometry

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.3 * ATR_14
